Title: Record canonical EvalOps vs Evaller product decision
Description: Create a short decision record choosing whether this repo should ship EvalOps Copilot, Evaller, or Evaller as a sub-flow inside EvalOps Copilot.
User Value: Prevents the team from spending implementation time on the wrong product surface.
Current State: prompt.md and audit docs describe EvalOps Copilot. Active routes and metadata describe Evaller.
Gap / Risk: Without a decision, route, test, data, and copy work will continue to drift.
Scope: Review existing docs and active routes. Write a decision record with chosen product name, top-level routes, active nav labels, public landing stance, and migration implications.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not implement route changes, rename code, delete either UI surface, or change database schema.
Definition of Done: A decision record exists and README/ROADMAP link to it or summarize the decision.
Acceptance Tests: A reviewer can identify the canonical product name, route set, and which UI layer is authoritative.
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Read prompt.md, README.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/PRODUCT.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and active route files. Produce a plan-only decision record for EvalOps vs Evaller with route/nav implications.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Implement the approved decision-record-only change. Update docs to point future agents at the canonical product surface. Do not change app behavior.
Notes: Human decision required before route implementation.

Title: Create canonical route and navigation contract
Description: Turn the product decision into a concrete route/navigation contract for the MVP.
User Value: Gives Codex small route tickets with no ambiguity.
Current State: Required EvalOps routes redirect to /workspace; active nav exposes only Workspace, Runs, Templates, Settings.
Gap / Risk: Route implementation tickets are otherwise under-specified.
Scope: Document public routes, authenticated routes, redirects, nav labels, active product views, and expected page headings.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not wire routes or change UI components.
Definition of Done: A route contract table exists and each MVP route has an intended component/source.
Acceptance Tests: The contract lists /dashboard, /projects, /trace-import, /eval-builder, /graders, /prompt-optimizer, /routing-caching, /reports, /settings, /workspace, /runs, and /templates.
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Using the approved product decision, plan the canonical route/navigation contract. Keep this to docs only.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Add the approved route/navigation contract to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and update ROADMAP.md dependencies. Do not change app route behavior.
Notes: Blocks all route wiring tickets.

Title: Wire /dashboard to the canonical dashboard view
Description: Replace the current redirect for /dashboard with the approved canonical MVP view.
User Value: Users can open Eval Health Overview directly instead of being collapsed into /workspace.
Current State: src/app/dashboard/page.tsx redirects to /workspace.
Gap / Risk: The route appears in the build but does not expose the required MVP workflow.
Scope: Wire only /dashboard. Reuse existing approved components and shell patterns. Add or update one focused route assertion.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not refactor broad UI components, change unrelated routes, or alter backend behavior.
Definition of Done: /dashboard renders the approved Eval Health Overview experience and no longer blindly redirects to /workspace.
Acceptance Tests: Browser smoke can visit /dashboard and find the approved page heading/primary action.
Validation Commands: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test:e2e -- --grep "Eval Health Overview"
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Plan a minimal route-only change for /dashboard based on the approved route contract. Identify exact component reuse and test updates.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Implement the approved /dashboard route wiring only. Preserve existing app functionality and run focused validation.
Notes: Small route slice split out of the original broad MVP-pages roadmap item.

Title: Document EvalOps vs Evaller data ownership boundary
Description: Create a docs-only ADR describing whether EvalOps project tables or Evaller ai_test tables own the MVP workflow.
User Value: Prevents persistence, export, RLS, and reporting work from crossing models accidentally.
Current State: Both data models exist in code and migrations.
Gap / Risk: Duplicated concepts can cause confusing exports, auth checks, and route integration.
Scope: Compare model entities, identify canonical owners, and document migration/non-migration decisions.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not write migrations, drop tables, or change stores.
Definition of Done: docs/DATA_MODEL.md or a linked ADR names canonical owners for project, trace, eval, grader, report, run, and prompt entities.
Acceptance Tests: A reviewer can map each active MVP view to a data model without guessing.
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Plan a docs-only data ownership ADR for EvalOps vs Evaller. Do not propose destructive migrations.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Implement the approved docs-only data ownership ADR and update references.
Notes: Architecture decision, not implementation.

Title: Re-audit env inventory against runtime checks
Description: Verify that every app-owned process.env reference is represented in .env.example and readiness docs.
User Value: Operators can configure preview/production without leaking or missing secrets.
Current State: .env.example was updated in the audit, but it has not been re-checked after Linear backlog generation.
Gap / Risk: A missing or mislabeled env var can break production smoke or expose sensitive config.
Scope: Run env reference search, compare to .env.example, document any intentional Supabase template-only variables.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not add real secrets or change runtime behavior.
Definition of Done: .env.example and docs mention every required app-owned env var and no real values.
Acceptance Tests: Search output has no unexplained app-owned env vars missing from .env.example.
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Plan a narrow env inventory verification. Identify app-owned variables and docs-only/provider-template variables.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Update .env.example/docs only for missing env inventory items. Do not add real values.
Notes: Safe docs/config task.

Title: Run preview production smoke with real vendor credentials
Description: Configure preview secrets outside the repo and run the existing production smoke script against a preview deployment.
User Value: Confirms real Supabase, OpenAI, Inngest, Storage, billing gates, and tenant isolation work outside deterministic mode.
Current State: npm run smoke:production fails fast because required env vars are missing.
Gap / Risk: Production path may be broken even though deterministic tests pass.
Scope: Run smoke, capture exact failures, and create follow-up issues for failures.
Explicit Non-Goals: Do not commit credentials or patch product code in this ticket unless smoke script itself has a narrow bug.
Dependencies: Preview deployment and required smoke/vendor secrets.
Definition of Done: Preview smoke passes or each failure has a small follow-up issue with logs/root cause.
Acceptance Tests: EVALOPS_BASE_URL=<preview> npm run smoke:production completes successfully.
Suggested Codex Planning Prompt: Plan the preview smoke run: required env vars, expected flow, failure capture, and non-secret reporting.
Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Run the approved preview smoke procedure with real env outside the repo. Document failures only; do not commit secrets.
Notes: Blocked until human provides deployment/secrets access.

Suggested Codex Implementation Prompt: Implement only the approved auth test/docs fixes discovered during verification.
Notes: Requires preview credentials for full validation.
